comment_text.sn22.39:1.3: “Disillusionment” (nibbidā) is the turning away in disappointment upon realizing that one cannot get what one wants (MN 50:31.15, SN 35.240:1.8).

root_text.sn22.39:1.3: yaṁ rūpe nibbidābahulo vihareyya, vedanāya nibbidābahulo vihareyya, saññāya nibbidābahulo vihareyya, saṅkhāresu nibbidābahulo vihareyya, viññāṇe nibbidābahulo vihareyya.
root_text.sn22.39:1.4: Yo rūpe nibbidābahulo viharanto, vedanāya … saññāya … saṅkhāresu nibbidābahulo viharanto, viññāṇe nibbidābahulo viharanto rūpaṁ parijānāti, vedanaṁ … saññaṁ … saṅkhāre … viññāṇaṁ parijānāti,
root_text.sn22.39:1.5: so rūpaṁ parijānaṁ, vedanaṁ … saññaṁ … saṅkhāre … viññāṇaṁ parijānaṁ parimuccati rūpamhā, parimuccati vedanāya, parimuccati saññāya, parimuccati saṅkhārehi, parimuccati viññāṇamhā,
root_text.sn22.39:1.6: parimuccati jātiyā jarāmaraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi, ‘parimuccati dukkhasmā’ti vadāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.39:1.2: “Mendicants, when a mendicant is practicing in line with the teaching, this is what’s in line with the teaching.
translation_text.sn22.39:1.3: They should live full of disillusionment for form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.39:1.4: Living in this way, they completely understand form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.39:1.5: Completely understanding form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness, they’re freed from these things.
translation_text.sn22.39:1.6: They’re freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress. They’re freed from suffering, I say.”
